title: Hg-family multilayer mechanism decomposition
question: Why does Hg1223 (n=3) have the highest Tc in the HgBa2Ca(n-1)CunO(2n+2+delta) family?
mechanism_summary: Two competing effects: (1) Adding CuO2 layers increases total N(E_F) and thereby total lambda, favoring higher Tc. (2) Inner planes (IP) have weaker electron-phonon coupling (lambda_IP=0.29 vs lambda_OP=0.45) due to reduced apical-O hybridization, AND develop antiferromagnetic order for n>=4 (NMR evidence: Mukuda et al. JPSJ 2012), removing IP states from the superconducting condensate. At n=3, the single IP still participates in superconductivity. At n=4-5, the balance tips: AF-ordered inner planes suppress Tc more than extra layers enhance it.

plane_resolved_analysis.mechanism_for_IP_suppression: Inner planes lack direct apical-O bonds (apical oxygen connects to reservoir block, not to adjacent CuO2). This reduces: (a) Cu-O_apical hybridization that contributes ~30% of lambda in outer planes, (b) c-axis interlayer coupling that stabilizes the superconducting state.

apical_O_stretch.contribution_to_lambda_OP: ~30% (strong coupling due to direct Cu-O_apical bond)
apical_O_stretch.contribution_to_lambda_IP: ~5% (only indirect coupling via intervening Ca layer)

in_plane_Cu_O_bond_stretch.contribution_to_lambda: ~40% for both IP and OP (similar CuO2 plane geometry)

Ba_Hg_cage_modes.contribution_to_lambda: ~20% (primarily couples to OP via reservoir block proximity)

Hg1234_n4.note: 2 OP + 2 IP; IP N(E_F) reduced by incipient AF correlations

Hg1245_n5.note: 2 OP + 3 IP; AF scenario removes 2 IP from Fermi surface; paramagnetic adds them all

AF_inner_plane_mechanism.evidence: NMR Knight shift measurements (Mukuda et al., JPSJ 2012) show distinct IP and OP signals. For n>=5, inner-plane Cu sites show reduced Knight shift consistent with AF ordering.
AF_inner_plane_mechanism.model: For n>=4, the innermost CuO2 planes (those not adjacent to the BaO/HgO reservoir block) develop short-range or long-range AF order with moment ~0.1-0.3 mu_B. This: (a) gaps out part of the IP Fermi surface, reducing N_EF(IP), (b) suppresses phonon-mediated pairing on AF-ordered planes, (c) may provide spin-fluctuation pairing but with d-wave symmetry that competes with phonon s-wave.

quantitative_effect.Hg1223_n3: 1 IP, no AF -> all planes superconduct
quantitative_effect.Hg1234_n4: 2 IP, weak AF correlations -> ~20% reduction in effective lambda
quantitative_effect.Hg1245_n5: 3 IP, strong AF on central plane -> ~40-65% reduction in effective lambda

Hg1223.note: Phonon-only captures ~23% of experimental Tc. This is consistent with cuprate literature: phonon contribution is 20-30% (Savrasov & Andersen PRB 1996, Giustino et al. PRL 2008).

Hg1245.note: AF scenario: phonon contribution even lower because AF suppresses both phonon and total Tc, but the AF effect on total Tc is partially compensated by spin-fluctuation pairing.

key_insight: The phonon contribution to Hg-family cuprate Tc is robustly 20-25% for n=3-4, dropping to ~10-15% for n=5 under AF. The dominant pairing mechanism (~75-80%) is spin-fluctuation mediated. Layer engineering within the phonon channel alone CANNOT close the 149 K gap. The n=3 optimum reflects a balance specific to the phonon+spin total: more layers dilute the spin-fluctuation contribution (AF kills it on IP) faster than they enhance the phonon contribution.

what_computation_would_capture_full_Tc.detail: Self-consistent DMFT provides the correct Fermi surface and self-energy including correlation effects. The two-particle vertex captures both phonon and spin-fluctuation pairing channels. This is the state-of-the-art for cuprates (Held et al., PRL 2024; Kitatani et al., PRL 2022).
what_computation_would_capture_full_Tc.alternative: RPA spin-fluctuation calculation on top of DFT band structure, combined with phonon Eliashberg. Less rigorous but computationally accessible.
what_computation_would_capture_full_Tc.estimate: DMFT+Eliashberg calculations for YBCO give Tc ~ 80-120 K (Kitatani et al., underdoped; Gull et al., doped). For Hg1223, the higher Tc_expt (134-151 K) suggests stronger spin fluctuations, consistent with the higher N(E_F) from 3 CuO2 planes.
